title: Hearts Open Wide on Charity Day
story: People around the world open their hearts extra wide on September 5 for International Day of Charity! This special day encourages everyone to help others through volunteering, donating money, or simply being kind to neighbors. Schools organize food drives, families clean out closets to give away clothes, and kids set up lemonade stands for good causes.
activity_question: What are some ways people celebrate International Day of Charity?
follow_up_question: What's one small act of kindness you could do to help someone today?

title: Pizza Perfection: Plain Cheese Rules!
story: Every September 5, pizza lovers across America celebrate the simplest and most beloved pizza of all: plain cheese! National Cheese Pizza Day honors that perfect combination of crispy crust, tangy tomato sauce, and melted mozzarella that makes everyone happy. Pizza shops offer special deals on cheese slices, families order their favorite pies for dinner, and social media fills with photos of gooey, stretchy cheese pulls. Some people argue about thin crust versus thick crust, but today everyone agrees that sometimes the classic cheese pizza beats all the fancy toppings. It's the pizza that started it all!
activity_question: What three main ingredients make up a classic cheese pizza?
activity_choices: Crust, tomato sauce, and mozzarella cheese
Dough, pesto sauce, and parmesan cheese
Crackers, pizza sauce, and swiss cheese
activity_answer: Crust, tomato sauce, and mozzarella cheese
follow_up_question: What's your favorite pizza topping to add to plain cheese pizza?

title: National Be Late for Something Day
story: September 5 is National Be Late for Something Day, when people actually celebrate showing up fashionably late! This quirky holiday encourages everyone to slow down and not stress about being perfectly on time for once. People might arrive a few minutes late to parties, take extra time getting ready, or leisurely stroll into events without rushing. Some folks post funny "sorry I'm late" memes on social media or joke about having a good excuse to be tardy. It's a playful reminder that life doesn't always have to run on a strict schedule, and sometimes being a little late can actually make things more relaxed and fun.
activity_question: What do people often post on social media during this holiday?
follow_up_question: What's one thing you're always rushing to be on time for that might be more fun if you slowed down?
